workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Landmine Neutral Grip Chest Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
instructions: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
Position: Lie supine on the floor (or on a flat bench) with knees bent and feet flat; align your chest under the handles so the bar starts near your sternum.
Grip: Reach up and grasp the neutral handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands roughly shoulder-width apart.
Brace: Take a breath, brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Press: Drive the handles upward and slightly forward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (do not force a hard lockout).
Pause & control: Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position under control until they touch or come close to your chest.
Reps & safety notes: Repeat for the desired reps. Keep feet planted, avoid excessive low-back arch, and maintain scapular control throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A landmine chest press performed from a supine position using a Viking press attachment and a neutral (palms-facing) grip. It emphasizes horizontal pressing mechanics to load the pectorals while the neutral grip reduces shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
instructions: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
Grip the bar with a close/narrow grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower), retract your shoulder blades, and unrack the bar to hold it over your chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar under control to your chest, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body, and allow the bar to briefly rest on your chest (dead stop).
Press the bar back up by driving through your chest and triceps until arms are fully extended; maintain core tension throughout since you have no leg drive.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A close-grip variation of the bench press performed in the Larsen style with legs extended straight on the bench rather than planted on the floor. This eliminates leg drive and increases core stability demands while the narrow grip emphasizes triceps involvement alongside the pectorals and front delts.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on the floor with legs extended or knees bent, holding the barbell with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip at about shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Lie supine on the floor with legs extended or knees bent, holding the barbell with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip at about shoulder-width apart.
Position the bar directly over your chest with arms extended, keeping wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower the bar in a controlled manner until your upper arms lightly touch the floor, keeping elbows at roughly 45 degrees to your torso.
Pause briefly when arms contact the floor, then press the bar back up by extending the elbows until arms are fully extended.
Keep your core braced throughout and exhale during the pressing phase; use a spotter when handling heavier loads.
exercises.description: A floor press performed with a supinated (underhand/reverse) grip that places greater emphasis on the upper chest while still engaging the triceps and front delts. Performed lying supine on the floor with legs extended, the bar is lowered until the upper arms touch the floor, then pressed back to full arm extension. The floor limits range of motion, reducing shoulder stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
Grasp a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight over your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your hips down to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by hinging at the shoulders; keep a slight bend in the elbows and do not let them flare excessively.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in the chest/lats or until the bar is roughly level with the crown of your head — do not go so far that your lower back overarches.
Exhale and pull the barbell back in the same arc to the start position over your chest by contracting the pectorals and lats; keep the movement smooth and controlled.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full control on both lowering and lifting phases.
Use a spotter or lighter weight if you cannot control the bar safely behind your head; stop if you feel sharp shoulder or back pain.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the barbell pullover moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a deep stretch and contraction of the chest while also engaging the lats and triceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Incline Bench Row
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45 degrees and position it so your chest rests firmly on the pad with feet braced on the floor or foot supports (prone position).
Load a barbell and grasp it with a shoulder-width reverse (underhand) grip so palms face up and arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Start with your chest pressed into the bench, shoulders relaxed and scapulae slightly protracted, core engaged and neck neutral.

Initiate the movement by retracting your scapulae and pulling the barbell upward in a controlled path toward your lower chest / upper abdomen, leading with the elbows and keeping them close to your sides.
Pause 1 second at the top and squeeze the lats and upper back without hyperextending the spine or lifting your chest off the bench.
Lower the barbell slowly back to the start with full arm extension while maintaining tension in the lats; avoid dropping the weight or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired reps. Breathe out while pulling and inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: Prone incline bench row performed with a reverse (underhand) grip that emphasizes the lats while also engaging the upper back and arms. Lying chest-down on an incline bench, you pull a barbell up toward the lower chest/upper abdomen and lower it under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the midfoot; grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width using an overhand or mixed grip.
Hinge at the hips, push the butt back, and bend the knees slightly so the torso is angled forward (near parallel to the floor), keeping a neutral spine and head in line with the spine.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to set the upper back before the first rep.
Pull the barbell upward toward the lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and keeping them close to the body; focus on squeezing the upper back at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a flat back, then lower the barbell under control until arms are nearly extended while preserving the hip-hinge position.
Breathe out while pulling the bar up and inhale while lowering it; keep the torso stable and avoid rounding the lower back or using excessive torso momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, and reset your hip-hinge position between sets if you lose posture.
exercises.description: A hip-hinged barbell row that trains the upper back by pulling the loaded barbell toward the torso while maintaining a neutral spine. It also recruits the lats, arms and posterior chain for stability and power.

exercises.name: Barbell Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and your head and upper back supported; grip the barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and your head and upper back supported; grip the barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the barbell held directly over your chest with your arms nearly extended and a slight bend in the elbows; brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc backwards and over your head until you feel a stretch through the chest and lats (keep elbows slightly bent and fixed throughout the motion).
Pause briefly at the bottom of the pullover, then pull the bar back in the same arc to bring it over your chest by contracting your lats and pectorals.
From the chest position, press the barbell straight up overhead until arms are nearly extended to complete the press portion, then lower the bar back to the chest in control.
Repeat the pullover-to-press sequence for the desired reps; maintain core tension, avoid overarching the lower back, and use a spotter for heavier loads.
exercises.description: A supine barbell movement that combines a pullover (bar travels in an arc behind the head) with a press back to overhead, emphasizing a stretch and contraction of the chest while also recruiting the lats and triceps.

exercises.name: Lever Alternating Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so the back pad supports your upper back and the lever handles sit just above/behind your head when seated upright.
Load an appropriate plate weight and sit with your back flat against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent; brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp the right and left lever handles (one in each hand) with a neutral or overhand grip. Begin with both arms extended overhead with a slight bend in the elbows.

For each rep, exhale and pull one lever down and in a smooth arc toward your chest/upper stomach using the shoulder joint — lead with the upper arm, not the wrist, and keep the elbow slightly bent and fixed.
At the bottom of the movement, pause briefly and focus on squeezing the pectorals and lats on the working side while keeping your torso stationary.
Inhale and slowly return the lever under control back to the start (overhead) to complete the eccentric phase; avoid letting the shoulder snap back.
Alternate sides each repetition (right arm then left arm) while maintaining the same tempo, core bracing, and no excessive torso rotation or lumbar arch.
Keep movements controlled, avoid shrugging the shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain a fixed elbow angle and stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover is a single-arm/alternating machine pullover that moves the upper arm in an arc from overhead down toward the chest, emphasizing the pectorals while also recruiting the lats and serratus anterior. The movement is performed with a slight elbow bend and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Arm Pullover
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor and your torso flat against the bench (supine position).
Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight up over your chest with elbows bent roughly 90 degrees; keep the elbows fixed throughout the movement.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to stabilize the shoulders before beginning the descent.

Slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by allowing shoulder extension (keeping the elbow angle constant) until you feel a stretch in the lats/upper chest area or until the bar reaches a comfortable range of motion.

Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the lats and serratus anterior, then pull the bar back over the chest by driving the shoulders into flexion using your lats and chest (exhale on the concentric).
Keep the movement smooth and controlled, avoiding excessive lower back arch; maintain the bent-elbow position and avoid turning the elbows outward or using momentum.
Perform the desired number of reps and then safely rack the barbell, or have a spotter assist when lowering the weight off your chest.
exercises.description: A supine barbell pullover performed with the elbows bent that moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, stretching and loading the latissimus region while also recruiting the chest and triceps. Performed with a fixed elbow angle to emphasize shoulder extension and thoracic mobility.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and torso vertical (back neutral).
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and torso vertical (back neutral).
Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and palms facing forward; rest the bar at upper chest/clavicle level.
Brace your core and squeeze your shoulder blades down and slightly back to stabilize the spine; keep head neutral with eyes forward.
Inhale, then press the bar upward in a vertical path by extending your elbows and driving through the delts until arms are nearly locked out overhead.
As the bar passes your forehead, slightly tilt your head back to allow clearance, then return your head to neutral at the top.
Pause briefly at the top with the bar roughly over mid-foot, shoulder blades stable and ribs down to avoid excessive lower-back arching.
Exhale and slowly lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest/clavicle, keeping elbows slightly in front of the body.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining a tight core and steady breathing; avoid bouncing the bar off the chest or flaring the ribs.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead press performed from a seated, upright position pressing a barbell from the upper chest/clavicle to full overhead lockout. Primarily targets the shoulder delts while engaging triceps and upper traps for assistance and stabilization.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a 30–45° angle if mobility requires), keeping a neutral spine and chest up.
Grip the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width so elbows can travel out to the sides.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades, maintaining a stable torso throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows outward and up, pulling the bar toward the upper chest/upper abdomen while keeping the elbows high to emphasize the rear delts.
At the top, pause briefly and squeeze the rear delts and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Slowly lower the bar under control back to the start position, fully extending the arms while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row variation that specifically emphasizes the rear delts by rowing with elbows flared out to the sides. Performed from a hip-hinged standing position to target the posterior shoulder and upper back.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and lift the bar by driving your elbows out to the sides and slightly back, squeezing the rear delts at the top.
Raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or slightly below shoulder height, pause briefly.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control, avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Standing upright rear delt raise using a barbell held behind the body. The bar is lifted by driving the elbows out and back to target the posterior deltoids and upper back while maintaining an upright torso position.

exercises.name: Barbell Rear Delt Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell behind your body with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Keep your chest up, core braced, and maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
With arms relatively straight, lift the barbell backward and upward away from your body by squeezing the rear delts.
Raise the bar as high as comfortable while maintaining control, then lower slowly back to the starting position.
Avoid leaning forward or using momentum; focus on controlled movement through the rear deltoids.
exercises.description: A standing barbell exercise performed with the bar held behind the body using an overhand grip. The movement involves lifting the barbell backward and upward away from the glutes by extending the shoulders, keeping arms relatively straight to target the posterior deltoids and traps.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Front Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and your back neutral (not rounded).
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and your back neutral (not rounded).
Grip a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower and let it rest on your thighs with a slight bend in the elbows.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades slightly down and back to avoid shrugging, and keep your torso still throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise the barbell straight forward in a controlled motion until it reaches shoulder or eye level; keep the elbows slightly bent and the wrists neutral.
Pause 1 second at the top without leaning back or using momentum, then inhale and lower the barbell slowly back to the starting position along the same path.
Maintain slow, controlled reps and avoid swinging the torso; if you find yourself swinging, reduce the weight or perform the movement standing with lighter load.
Focus on moving from the shoulders (delts) rather than pulling with the traps or arms; keep grip firm but avoid excessive wrist movement.
exercises.description: A seated front raise with a barbell that isolates the anterior delts by lifting the weight straight in front of the body from thigh level to about shoulder/eye height; performed seated to limit momentum and emphasize the front shoulder.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width; let the bar rest against your thighs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width; let the bar rest against your thighs.
Brace your abs, keep your chest up and spine neutral, and maintain a slight bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Exhale and raise the barbell straight in front of you by lifting at the shoulders until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor (shoulder height); lead the movement with your elbows.
Hold the top position briefly (0–1 seconds) while keeping the shoulders engaged and avoiding shrugging or excessive leaning back.
Inhale and lower the barbell slowly and under control back to the starting position at your thighs.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, using a weight light enough to avoid swinging or recruiting the legs/back; focus on slow, controlled motion.
exercises.description: A standing barbell front raise that emphasizes the anterior delts by lifting a barbell from the thighs to shoulder height with straight or slightly bent arms while keeping the torso stable to minimize momentum.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and spine neutral; brace your abs.
Grasp the barbell with a shoulder-width (or slightly narrower) overhand grip and press it straight overhead until your arms are fully extended; this is the start position.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing forward and close to your head, maintaining a stable shoulder position.

Inhale and slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending only at the elbows, keeping the upper arms stationary until your forearms are about parallel to the floor or you feel a stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly, then exhale and press the barbell back up by extending the elbows to return to the fully extended overhead position while keeping the core braced.
Avoid excessive lower-back arching or letting the elbows flare out; if necessary use lighter weight or a spotter to maintain safe form.
After completing your set, have a spotter help lower the bar to your chest/thighs before removing it from your lap, or carefully rack the bar with assistance.
exercises.description: Seated barbell overhead triceps extension is a single-joint exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows from an overhead position while the upper arms remain stationary. It emphasizes the long head of the triceps and requires core and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Close Grip Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart, knees slightly soft and core braced.
Grasp a barbell with a close underhand (supinated) grip — hands roughly 6–8 inches (inside shoulder width) — wrists neutral and palms facing up.
Let the arms hang fully extended so the bar rests at thigh level; pull shoulders slightly down and back to remove upper-back shrug.
Keeping the elbows pinned to your sides and stationary, exhale and curl the bar upward by contracting the biceps until the forearms approach the shoulders.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top (about 0–1 second) while keeping the wrists straight and avoiding shoulder movement.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to the start position until the arms are fully extended.
Avoid swinging the torso or using hip drive; if you must lean back to lift, reduce the weight. Repeat for desired repetitions.
exercises.description: A standing barbell curl performed with a narrow, supinated grip to emphasize the biceps by shortening the elbow flexors while reducing shoulder involvement. Performed standing with controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Barbell Finger Curls
exercises.notes: Lean forward and place your forearms on your thighs or on the bench so your wrists hang just past the edge; palms should face up (supinated).
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees about hip-width apart and a neutral spine.
Lean forward and place your forearms on your thighs or on the bench so your wrists hang just past the edge; palms should face up (supinated).
Grip the barbell with the fingertips so the bar rests on the pads of your fingers (not fully in the palm) and arms remain pressed against thighs/bench to isolate the fingers.
Keep your wrists and elbows stationary; curl the bar by flexing your fingers and rolling the bar into the palm until you feel a full contraction in the forearm/finger flexors.
Squeeze briefly at the top, then slowly extend the fingers to roll the bar back out to the starting fingertip position under control.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, breathing out during the curl and inhaling as you release; use a light-to-moderate weight to avoid excessive wrist strain.
exercises.description: Seated exercise where the forearms rest on the thighs or bench and the barbell is curled using the fingers to train the finger/wrist flexors and improve grip strength. Movement involves rolling or curling the bar up into the palms using the fingertips and then slowly releasing it back out.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Back Wrist Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
Hold a barbell behind your thighs with a pronated (overhand) grip so the backs of your hands face backward and your arms are fully extended at your sides.
Keep your elbows fixed and close to your torso — only the wrists should move.
Exhale and extend (lift) your wrists so the back of each hand moves toward the forearm, raising the barbell using your wrist extensors.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement while maintaining a tight grip and stable elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position until you feel a controlled stretch in the forearms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding shoulder or elbow movement and using a controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Standing back wrist curls isolate the wrist extensor muscles by moving the barbell with the backs of the hands while the arms remain stationary at the sides. The bar is held behind the body and the wrists are extended and lowered under control.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Full Squat
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
instructions: Stand upright (standing) under a loaded barbell set at about mid-chest height in a squat rack; step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps/shoulders (not on the neck).
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width, lift the chest, pull the shoulder blades together, and unrack the bar by straightening the legs and stepping back to clear the rack.
Position feet shoulder-width to slightly wider with toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable stance with weight distributed through the midfoot and heel.
Brace your core (abs) and keep a neutral spine; inhale before you descend to maintain intra-abdominal pressure.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously, keeping the chest up and spine neutral — descend until your hips drop below parallel (full squat depth).
Ensure knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward; keep the weight on the heels/midfoot and maintain even foot contact with the floor.
Pause briefly at the bottom if desired, then drive forcefully through the heels to extend the hips and knees and return to a fully upright position, exhaling as you ascend.
Control the movement on the way up and down; use safety pins or a spotter when training heavy and avoid rounding the lower back at any point.
exercises.description: A barbell back full squat performed to below-parallel depth; primarily loads the quadriceps while also recruiting the glutes, hamstrings and core for stabilization. The lifter descends with the bar on the upper back and then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Landmine Sumo Squat
exercises.notes: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the landmine by anchoring one end of the bar in the pivot and loading the other end with the desired weight; grasp the bar with both hands close to the plates or use a V-handle attachment.
Stand facing the bar with a wide (sumo) stance: feet wider than shoulder-width, toes turned slightly outward and weight distributed through the heels and midfoot.
Hold the bar close to your body at chest level with elbows down and a neutral spine; brace your core (abs) and keep chest tall and head neutral.

Begin the descent by pushing your hips back and bending at the knees, letting them track outward over your toes; descend until your thighs are about parallel to the floor or a comfortable depth with no lumbar rounding.
Keep the bar close, maintain an upright torso, and feel tension in the glutes and adductors throughout the descent.
Drive back up by pressing through the heels and pushing your knees out, extending the hips and knees to stand while maintaining core bracing and chest position.
Control the bar on the way down for each rep; exhale as you drive up and inhale as you descend.
Maintain knee alignment (tracking toes), neutral spine, and avoid collapsing the knees inward or letting the torso fold forward.
exercises.description: A wide-stance squat performed with a landmine bar held in front of the body; the sumo stance shifts emphasis to the glutes and inner thighs while the landmine setup helps maintain an upright torso. It builds hip extension strength and targets adductors and quads as you drive up from the bottom position.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch (hands pad)
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine height and select appropriate weight, then sit with feet flat on the floor and hips pressed firmly against the back pad.
instructions: Adjust the machine height and select appropriate weight, then sit with feet flat on the floor and hips pressed firmly against the back pad.
Place your forearms across the padded lever arm with hands gripping the handles or resting on the pad, starting with torso upright.
Exhale and curl your torso forward by flexing at the spine, bringing your chest toward your pelvis while keeping hips stationary.
Pause and squeeze the abs at the bottom of the movement, avoiding pulling with the arms or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the upright starting position under control, maintaining tension in the abs throughout.
exercises.description: A seated lever crunch performed with the forearms resting on a padded lever arm to force trunk flexion and isolate the abdominal muscles. The movement is a controlled curl of the torso toward the pelvis while keeping the hips stationary.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever standing calf raise machine and step onto the foot platform so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can hang freely.
Position your shoulders under the padded lever, stand tall and grip the machine handles for stability; feet should be hip-width and toes pointing forward (or slightly turned in/out to vary emphasis).
Begin with a full stretch: lower your heels down until you feel a controlled stretch in the calves (keep torso upright and spine neutral).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, exhaling and keeping knees straight (a slight knee bend shifts emphasis to the soleus if desired).
Pause 1–2 seconds at the top and squeeze the calves, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched starting position with control—avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and proper alignment throughout.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing calf raise where the shoulders are braced under a padded lever and the heels drop and rise from a platform to load the calf muscles through a full range of motion. Emphasizes plantarflexion to strengthen and develop the gastrocnemius/soleus complex.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform so your heels can hang freely; grip the machine handles for stability.
instructions: Sit on the machine seat with knees bent (approximately 90°) and your lower thighs positioned under the padded lever so the pad rests comfortably on top of the thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform so your heels can hang freely; grip the machine handles for stability.
Load the lever arm with the desired weight plates and ensure the machine is secured before beginning.
Start with your heels lowered below the platform to get a full stretch in the calves.
Exhale and drive through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising your heels as high as possible by contracting the calf muscles.
Pause and squeeze the calves briefly at the top (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a neutral spine and firm grip on the handles.
Inhale and lower your heels slowly and with control back to the starting stretched position — avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the planned number of repetitions, maintaining a slow, controlled tempo and keeping the knees stationary under the pad throughout the set.
exercises.description: Seated calf raise performed on a plate-loaded lever machine; with knees bent and a padded lever pressing on the thighs, you plantarflex the ankles to raise the heels and lift the load, isolating the calves (mainly the soleus).

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use a double overhand or mixed grip just outside the legs.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use a double overhand or mixed grip just outside the legs.
Unrack the bar (or pick it up) and take a slight, fixed bend in the knees — keep the legs ‘stiff’ (not locked) throughout the movement.
Hinge at the hips by pushing the butt back while keeping a neutral spine and chest up; allow the bar to travel down close to the thighs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong stretch in the hamstrings (usually mid- to lower-shin level), keeping shoulders pulled back and neck neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive the hips forward and squeeze the glutes to return to a fully upright standing position.
Keep the movement controlled on the way down and avoid rounding the lower back; breathe in on the descent and exhale as you stand.
Use appropriate weight so form is maintained; stop the set if you cannot keep a neutral spine or if knees start to bend significantly.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge barbell lift that emphasizes lengthening and loading the hamstrings by lowering the bar with minimal knee bend, then returning to standing by driving the hips forward. Maintain a neutral spine and controlled tempo to target the posterior chain.

exercises.name: Barbell Lateral Lunge
exercises.notes: Start standing upright with a barbell resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck), feet hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, and core braced.
instructions: Start standing upright with a barbell resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck), feet hip-width apart, toes pointing forward, and core braced.
Take a controlled large step directly to the side with one foot, keeping the opposite foot stationary and weight centered over both hips.

As the stepping foot lands flat, push your hips back and bend the stepping knee to lower your torso into a lateral lunge; keep the trail leg relatively straight with a slight bend in the knee and toes facing forward.
Keep your chest up, spine neutral, and core engaged; allow the knee of the stepping leg to track in line with the toes (do not let it collapse inward).
Lower until the thigh of the stepping leg is approximately parallel to the floor or as mobility allows, feeling load in the quads, glutes, and adductors.
Drive through the heel of the stepping foot and actively push your feet into the floor to return to the starting stance, bringing the feet back together under control.
Repeat for the prescribed reps on one side, then perform the same number of reps on the opposite side. Breathe in on the step down and exhale as you push back to standing.
exercises.description: A loaded lateral stepping lunge that emphasizes the working leg's quadriceps while also engaging the glutes and inner thigh (adductors) for lateral strength and stability. The barbell rests across the upper back as you step out to the side, sit back into the hip and return to standing.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Hip Extension
exercises.notes: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Set the desired weight on the lever machine and position the lower-leg pad so it rests just above the ankle of the working leg.
Stand facing the machine and place your chest/forearms or hands on the support pad/handles for stability; plant the non-working foot flat on the floor and keep it slightly bent for balance.
Place the working foot against the lever pad behind you so the pad contacts the lower shin/ankle area, with the working knee either slightly bent or in a comfortable neutral angle.
Brace your core, keep your torso upright and hips square, and avoid overarching the lower back throughout the movement.
Exhale and drive the working leg straight back by extending the hip, squeezing the glutes at the top of the movement until the leg is in line with or slightly behind the torso.
Pause briefly at full extension while maintaining a tight core, then inhale and slowly return the lever to the starting position under control without letting the weight stack slam.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
exercises.description: A machine-based standing hip extension where one leg pushes a rear lever backward to extend the hip, emphasizing the glute muscles with assistance from the hamstrings. Performed while braced against a pad/handle to isolate hip extension through a controlled rearward kick.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your chest against the angled chest pad and your feet secured under the lower rollers; adjust the seat so the pad rests across your upper chest/shoulders comfortably.
Grasp the handles at chest level (or hold the pad if no handles) and set a moderate weight; maintain a neutral head position and brace your core.

Exhale and contract your abs to curl your torso forward and down, flexing the spine while keeping the hips stationary and using controlled motion — continue until you feel a full contraction of the abs.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement while keeping tension in the abs; do not pull with your arms or yank with momentum.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting upright position with a controlled motion, maintaining core engagement and avoiding hyperextension of the spine.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Adjust weight to maintain strict form and avoid using neck or hip drive to complete the movement.
exercises.description: A seated, machine-based crunch performed with a chest pad and lever; the exercise flexes the torso forward against resistance to target the rectus abdominis. The knees and feet are stabilized while the upper torso curls forward, emphasizing spinal flexion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Shoulder Grip Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a barbell with a pronated (palms facing body) shoulder-width grip so the bar rests against your thighs with arms fully extended.
Keep your chest up, shoulders down and back, and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, leading with the elbows rather than the hands so the bar travels close to your body.

Lift the bar to about upper-chest / collarbone level or until your elbows are roughly shoulder height; avoid shrugging the shoulders excessively or lifting above shoulder level to reduce impingement risk.
Pause briefly at the top with control, then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position under control.
Breathe out on the concentric (lifting) phase and inhale on the eccentric (lowering) phase.
Use a controlled tempo and moderate weight to preserve form; stop the set if you feel shoulder pain and reduce range of motion or load as needed.
exercises.description: Standing barbell upright row performed with a shoulder-width (pronated) grip; the movement elevates the elbows and lifts the bar vertically toward the upper chest/collarbone, primarily working the delts and engaging traps and arm muscles.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.

Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.

exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Power Snatch
exercises.notes: Stand facing the barbell which is positioned on blocks at about mid-thigh; feet hip- to shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out.
instructions: Stand facing the barbell which is positioned on blocks at about mid-thigh; feet hip- to shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out.
Use a wide snatch grip so the bar will sit overhead at full arm extension; grip the bar with straight wrists and engage your lats and abs to maintain a tight torso.
Hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend and keep chest up and back flat so the bar is over mid-foot; eyes forward and spine neutral.
Initiate the lift by explosively extending the hips and knees (triple extension) while keeping the bar close to your body.
As you extend, aggressively shrug and pull the bar upward with straight arms as high as possible, keeping the elbows pointing outward slightly.
Drop under the bar quickly into a partial (power) squat or quarter-squat position and punch the bar overhead to lock the arms out directly above the midline of the body.
Stand up tall from the receiving position with hips locked and shoulders shrugged under the bar, maintaining a neutral spine and engaged core.
Lower the bar under control back to the blocks (or to the floor if required) and reset your stance and grip before repeating.
Keep the bar path close, avoid excessive backward lean, and breathe out on the explosive pull while bracing your core throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A standing, explosive Olympic-style lift performed from raised blocks where the barbell is pulled overhead in one continuous motion and received in a shallow (power) squat or quarter position. The move emphasizes a rapid hip extension and an aggressive pull and shrug to get the bar overhead with locked arms.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

workout.description: Full Body workout focusing on strength training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline, lie back with your head, upper back, and glutes firmly against the pad, and plant feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing away, unrack the bar and position it directly above your upper chest with arms extended.
Inhale and lower the bar with control to your upper chest near the collarbone, keeping elbows at a 45-60 degree angle from your torso.
Press the bar back up explosively while exhaling, extending your arms fully without locking the elbows aggressively.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the barbell with control.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Larsen Press
exercises.notes: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
instructions: Set up on a flat bench with a barbell racked at appropriate height; lie supine with your legs extended straight out on the bench (Larsen position) rather than feet on the floor.
Grip the bar with a close/narrow grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower), retract your shoulder blades, and unrack the bar to hold it over your chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar under control to your chest, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body, and allow the bar to briefly rest on your chest (dead stop).
Press the bar back up by driving through your chest and triceps until arms are fully extended; maintain core tension throughout since you have no leg drive.
Repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A close-grip variation of the bench press performed in the Larsen style with legs extended straight on the bench rather than planted on the floor. This eliminates leg drive and increases core stability demands while the narrow grip emphasizes triceps involvement alongside the pectorals and front delts.

exercises.name: Barbell Pin Bench Press Conventional grip
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).
instructions: Set the safety pins in the power rack to a height that places the bar just above your chest when lying supine on the bench (bar will contact the pins at the bottom of the movement).

Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor, and a slight natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress the shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a conventional shoulder-width (slightly wider than shoulder) overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Begin with the bar resting on the pins (arms either slightly bent or fully extended depending on your setup). Brace your core and take a full breath in before initiating the press.
Press the bar upward from the pins in a controlled, explosive concentric motion until the arms are fully extended and elbows locked out while exhaling.
Lower the bar under control along the same path until it gently contacts the pins at the set height (do not bounce the bar off the pins), pause as needed on the pins to reset tension.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining scapular retraction, a stable foot drive, and controlled tempo on the descent.
exercises.description: A bench press variation performed inside a rack where the bar starts and finishes on safety pins; it emphasizes the press from a dead stop to overload the pectorals through the concentric phase. Performed with a conventional (shoulder‑width) grip to target the mid-chest while the triceps and anterior delts assist.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
instructions: Set a decline bench in a rack and load the barbell with an appropriate weight; place your feet securely under the decline foot pads.
Sit on the bench, then lie back so your head is lower than your hips (decline position) and hook your feet under the pads to prevent sliding.
Grip the bar with a slightly wider-than-shoulder-width overhand grip, wrists stacked over elbows and shoulder blades retracted and depressed.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar over your lower chest/upper sternum; have a spotter if lifting heavy.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to touch or come just above the lower chest/upper abdomen (lower sternum), keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle to the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until the arms are nearly straight (do not lock aggressively).
Maintain tightness through the chest and core, keep the scapulae retracted, and avoid excessive arching of the lower back throughout the set.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar safely with the help of a spotter when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell pressing movement performed on a decline bench that emphasizes the lower portion of the pectorals. Lying with the head lower than the hips, you lower the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen and press it back up.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Smith Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to an appropriate height, add desired weight, and set safety stops slightly below chest level.
Position a flat bench in the machine so the bar will line up with the mid-chest when you are lying down.
Lie supine on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and a slight natural arch in the low back; retract your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grasp the bar with a medium grip (hands slightly wider than shoulder-width) and ensure wrists are stacked over elbows; keep head on the bench.
Unrack the bar by rotating it or disengaging the safety, then lower the bar under control to the mid-chest/sternum while keeping elbows at about a 30–60° angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then press the bar up explosively until arms are nearly straight without locking the elbows aggressively.
Maintain a tight core and pressed scapulae throughout the movement for stability; avoid excessive flaring of the elbows or hyperextending the lower back.
Control the descent on each rep and re-rack the bar by rotating it back into the safety catches when finished.
exercises.description: A Smith machine variation of the flat bench press performed lying supine on a flat bench, pressing the fixed bar vertically to work the chest. The guided bar path reduces stabilization demand while emphasizing pressing strength in the pectorals.

exercises.name: Smith Single Arm Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Set the Smith bar to a low position (just above shins) and load an appropriate weight. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the Smith bar to a low position (just above shins) and load an appropriate weight. Stand beside the bar with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (slight knee bend allowed). Maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs — this is a standing, bent-over position.

Reach down and grasp the bar with one hand (use a pronated/overhand or supinated/underhand grip depending on emphasis), keeping the non-working arm free to brace on your thigh or rack if needed for stability.

Start with the shoulder blade long and the elbow extended. Inhale, then exhale as you pull the bar upward toward the side of your ribcage/hip, leading with the elbow and squeezing the shoulder blade toward the spine.
Pause for a brief second at the top with a full scapular retraction, then slowly lower the bar back to the start under control until the elbow is nearly extended.
Perform the desired reps on one side, then switch to the other arm. Keep the torso steady — avoid excessive rotation or lumbar rounding throughout the set.
Common form cues: keep chest open, neck neutral, drive the elbow straight back (not flared excessively), and use a controlled tempo to emphasize the lats and upper back rather than momentum.
exercises.description: A single-arm bent-over row performed on a Smith machine that emphasizes pulling the working arm toward the hip while the torso is hinged forward, targeting the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Keep a braced core and neutral spine while driving the elbow up and back to retract the shoulder blade.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your chest rests firmly against the pad and your feet are flat on the floor; the lever handles should be at about hip height when your arms hang down.
Sit with your chest pressed into the pad, grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, and keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement.
Start with the arms hanging at your sides (shoulders relaxed) and brace your core to prevent movement of the torso.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor (about 90 degrees), keeping the elbows slightly bent and the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the top while avoiding shoulder shrugging (do not lift with the neck or upper trapezius).
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position under control, maintaining chest contact with the pad and minimizing momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, choosing a weight that allows strict form without swinging.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported lateral raise performed on a lever machine to isolate the lateral deltoids; the chest pad prevents torso swing so the delts do the work. Arms are raised out to the sides with a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.
