workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/ENDURANCE
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and endurance training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

exercises.name: Chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)
exercises.notes: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
instructions: Start by grasping parallel handles overhead with a narrow neutral grip (palms facing each other) and arms fully extended while hanging from the bar; cross your ankles or keep feet together and maintain a slight hollow body with ribs down.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to remove slack before initiating the pull (scapular set).
Exhale and pull your elbows down and back, driving the elbows toward your hips while focusing on pulling with the lats rather than just the arms.
Continue the pull until your chin clears or reaches the level of the handles and your chest approaches the grips; pause and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1 second.
Inhale and lower yourself slowly and under control back to a full hang with arms fully extended, maintaining shoulder tension and avoiding a sudden drop.
Keep the torso stable and avoid excessive swinging or kipping; if needed, use a controlled bend in the knees or a slight leg cross to prevent momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, focusing on smooth tempo, full range of motion, and consistent breathing.
exercises.description: A narrow, neutral-grip chin-up performed from a vertical hang that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while also engaging the arms and upper back. Pull the body up until the chin clears the handles, then lower under control to a full hang.

exercises.name: Landmine Neutral Grip Chest Press with Viking Press Attachment
exercises.notes: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
instructions: Set up: Anchor the landmine bar in the pivot and attach the Viking press handles; load the desired weight.
Position: Lie supine on the floor (or on a flat bench) with knees bent and feet flat; align your chest under the handles so the bar starts near your sternum.
Grip: Reach up and grasp the neutral handles with a palms-facing (neutral) grip, hands roughly shoulder-width apart.
Brace: Take a breath, brace your core and retract your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable pressing platform.
Press: Drive the handles upward and slightly forward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight (do not force a hard lockout).
Pause & control: Pause briefly at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position under control until they touch or come close to your chest.
Reps & safety notes: Repeat for the desired reps. Keep feet planted, avoid excessive low-back arch, and maintain scapular control throughout the movement.
exercises.description: A landmine chest press performed from a supine position using a Viking press attachment and a neutral (palms-facing) grip. It emphasizes horizontal pressing mechanics to load the pectorals while the neutral grip reduces shoulder strain.

exercises.name: Wide Hand Push-up
exercises.notes: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.
instructions: Start in a high-plank position with hands placed wider than shoulder-width (about 1.5x shoulder width), fingers splayed, and body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Brace your core and glutes to maintain a rigid body position with neutral spine and neck.
Lower your body by bending the elbows outward until your chest is close to the floor or elbows reach approximately 90 degrees.
Hold this lowered position isometrically, maintaining full-body tension and keeping hips level without sagging or piking.
Breathe steadily while holding for the desired duration, then press back up to complete the set.
exercises.description: An isometric hold variation of the wide hand push-up where you maintain a static lowered position with hands placed wider than shoulder width. This hold maximizes time under tension for the pectorals and core stabilizers while maintaining a rigid body line.

exercises.name: Barbell Decline Pullover
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet under the pads so your body is stable and supine on the bench.
Grasp a barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip about shoulder-width apart and lift it straight over your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Brace your core and keep your hips down to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell in a controlled arc behind your head by hinging at the shoulders; keep a slight bend in the elbows and do not let them flare excessively.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in the chest/lats or until the bar is roughly level with the crown of your head — do not go so far that your lower back overarches.
Exhale and pull the barbell back in the same arc to the start position over your chest by contracting the pectorals and lats; keep the movement smooth and controlled.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full control on both lowering and lifting phases.
Use a spotter or lighter weight if you cannot control the bar safely behind your head; stop if you feel sharp shoulder or back pain.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the barbell pullover moves the weight in an arc from over the chest to behind the head, emphasizing a deep stretch and contraction of the chest while also engaging the lats and triceps.

exercises.name: Lever Pullover (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
instructions: Step 1: Sit on the machine with your back pressed firmly against the backrest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Step 2: Adjust the seat and pivot so the lever handles start just above and behind your head when your arms are extended; select an appropriate weight.
Step 3: Reach up and grasp the handles with a comfortable (typically neutral or slightly pronated) grip, keeping a small, fixed bend in the elbows.
Step 4: Begin with the shoulders stretched overhead and the chest lifted slightly, maintaining a stable torso against the pad.
Step 5: Exhale and pull the lever in a controlled arc down and back toward your hips/upper thighs, leading with the elbows and focusing on squeezing the lats.
Step 6: Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement with elbows by your sides and a full lat contraction; avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Step 7: Inhale and slowly return the lever to the start position, resisting the weight and keeping tension on the lats without letting the shoulders fully relax.
Step 8: Repeat for desired reps; maintain strict form—no torso rocking, keep core engaged, and avoid locking the elbows at the end ranges.
exercises.description: Seated plate-loaded lever pullover that moves the arms in an overhead-to-hip arc to lengthen and contract the lats while also engaging the pectorals and serratus anterior. Performed on a lever machine with the torso supported by a backrest for stability.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

workout.title: Chest/Back - BODYBUILD/HIT
workout.description: Chest/Back workout focusing on bodybuild and hit training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Barbell Reverse Grip Bench Press on Floor
exercises.notes: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with legs extended and grasp the barbell with a reverse (supinated/underhand) grip at shoulder-width.
Unrack or lift the bar to position it directly over your chest with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar slowly until your upper arms touch or nearly touch the floor, keeping elbows tucked at about 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up to full arm extension, squeezing the chest at the top.
Maintain a flat back against the floor throughout the movement and control the tempo on each rep.
exercises.description: A floor-based bench press performed with a reverse (underhand/supinated) grip that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while limiting range of motion. Lying on the floor, you lower the bar to the chest/upper torso and press it back up under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Barbell Paused Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (or at a comfortable angled bend); keep the back flat and head in neutral alignment.
Grip the bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip (or mixed grip if needed for heavy loads) and brace your core.
Initiate the movement by pulling your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows back, bringing the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Hold and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top for 1–2 seconds (the paused portion) while keeping the torso stable and avoiding excess torso rotation.
Lower the bar under control until arms are extended and the bar is near knee level or a natural hang, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for desired reps while breathing out during the pull and inhaling as you lower; avoid rounding the back or using excessive momentum.
exercises.description: A bent-over barbell row performed from a hip-hinged position with a brief pause at the top of the pull to maximize scapular retraction and time under tension. Emphasizes rowing the elbows back while keeping the torso stable and the spine neutral.

exercises.name: Landmine Bent over Row with V-bar (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up: anchor one end of the barbell in the landmine pivot and load plates on the free end; attach a V-handle to the bar near the plates.
instructions: Set up: anchor one end of the barbell in the landmine pivot and load plates on the free end; attach a V-handle to the bar near the plates.
Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell between your legs; slightly bend the knees and hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from horizontal (bent-over position).
Keep a neutral spine and engaged core; shoulders should be slightly protracted with arms extended to start and palms facing each other on the V-handle (neutral grip).
Initiate the movement by retracting the shoulder blades and driving the elbows back and up, pulling the V-handle toward your lower ribs/upper abdomen while keeping the torso stable.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the top for a brief pause, ensuring elbows travel close to the body and posterior delts/traps assist the movement.
Lower the bar under control by extending the arms until near full extension while maintaining the hip hinge, neutral spine, and tension in the core and posterior chain.
Breathe out during the pull and inhale as you return to the start; avoid rounding the back, excessive torso swing, or using momentum from the hips.
exercises.description: A two-handed neutral-grip row performed with a V-handle on a landmine/barbell pivot, using a hip hinge to pull the bar toward the lower rib/upper abdomen to emphasize the lats and upper back while maintaining a strong torso position.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Lever Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so your upper arms (triceps) rest flat and comfortably on the angled arm pad and the elbow pivot lines up with the machine pivot; set an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so your upper arms (triceps) rest flat and comfortably on the angled arm pad and the elbow pivot lines up with the machine pivot; set an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest slightly forward against the pad if present, and grip the handles with a neutral or overhand grip; wrists should be straight.
Start with your elbows bent and the handles close to your face or chest so the triceps are shortened but not crammed; keep your upper arms pressed to the pad and stationary throughout.
Exhale and press the handles by extending your elbows until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking the elbows aggressively); focus on contracting the triceps.
Hold the extended position for a brief count (0–1 seconds) then inhale and slowly control the return to the start position by allowing the elbows to bend.
Keep the movement slow and controlled, avoid using body momentum or leaning the torso; maintain neutral wrists and avoid rotating the shoulders.
Repeat for the desired reps. If you feel shoulder movement or the upper arm leaving the pad, reduce weight or re‑adjust the seat for correct alignment.
exercises.description: Seated lever triceps extension is an isolation exercise performed on a seated lever machine where the elbows rest on a pad and the forearms push the handles to extend the elbows, emphasing the triceps. The movement keeps the upper arms fixed while extending and flexing at the elbow joint.

exercises.name: Lever Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
instructions: Set the machine: choose an appropriate weight, adjust the seat height so the pivot of the lever lines up with your shoulder joint and the handles start near your hips.
Sit upright with your back pressed into the seat pad, feet flat on the floor, and chest up; grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly pronated grip.
Start with your elbows slightly bent and arms resting at your sides (not locked), maintaining a neutral wrist and steady grip.
Exhale and lift the handles out to your sides in a controlled arc, leading with the elbow so the upper arm reaches about parallel to the floor (shoulder height).
Keep your torso still and avoid shrugging your shoulders; use your delts to move the weight rather than momentum or back extension.
Pause briefly at the top with the elbows slightly higher than the wrists, feeling tension in the delts and traps for scapular stability.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the start position with control, maintaining the slight elbow bend.
Repeat for the desired number of reps. Prioritize controlled tempo, full range without swinging, and maintain scapular stability throughout.
exercises.description: Seated lever lateral raise is a machine exercise that isolates the lateral head of the delts by abducting the arms out to the sides from a seated, supported position. It emphasizes controlled movement to raise the arms to about shoulder height while minimizing trunk movement.

exercises.name: Lever Hammer Grip Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your upper arms rest flat and comfortably on the preacher pad with elbows just over the pad edge; select an appropriate weight on the lever machine.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your upper arms rest flat and comfortably on the preacher pad with elbows just over the pad edge; select an appropriate weight on the lever machine.
Sit upright with chest against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and maintain a neutral (thumbs-up) hammer grip on the handles.
Start with your arms extended toward the lever’s low position while keeping a slight bend in the elbows (do not lock joints).
Exhale and curl the lever upward by flexing at the elbow, keeping your upper arms pressed to the pad and moving only the forearms.
Pause briefly at the top when you feel a full biceps contraction (handles near shoulder level), avoiding shoulder shrugging or body swing.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start position under control until your arms are nearly extended, maintaining tension on the muscles.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, focusing on smooth tempo and strict form; if you feel torso movement, reduce weight.
exercises.description: Seated lever preacher curl using a neutral (hammer) grip to isolate the biceps while emphasizing the brachioradialis/forearm muscles; performed on a preacher pad to minimize shoulder involvement and momentum.

exercises.name: Lever Pronated Grip Seated Scapula Retraction Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and load the machine so you can reach the handles with your arms extended and feet flat on the footrests; sit upright with a neutral spine and knees bent.
Grasp the handles with a pronated (overhand) grip and let your shoulders relax down and slightly forward to set the start position.
Initiate the movement by pulling the handles back using your shoulder blades: actively retract (pinch) the scapulae together while keeping the torso stable and chest neutral.
As you retract the scapulae, elevate your shoulders into a controlled shrug (shoulder elevation) and squeeze your traps and upper back at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Slowly reverse the motion: lower the shoulders and allow the scapulae to protract back to the start position under control — do not let the weight slam forward.
Keep the arms relatively straight with a slight bend at the elbows, avoid using momentum or torso swing, and breathe out during the contraction and in during the return.
exercises.description: Seated, pronated-grip plate-loaded exercise that emphasizes scapular retraction and an upward shrug to target the trapezius and upper back. Performed from a seated position using the machine handles to actively pinch the shoulder blades together and elevate the shoulders.

exercises.name: Lever Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Set the machine weight and adjust the seat so your upper arms/elbows align with the machine's pivot and your back is flat against the pad.
instructions: Set the machine weight and adjust the seat so your upper arms/elbows align with the machine's pivot and your back is flat against the pad.
Sit upright with feet flat on the floor, chest up, and lean slightly into the back pad; grasp the lever handles with a supinated (palms-up) grip.
Keep your elbows fixed at your sides (or against the pad) and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the handles toward your shoulders by flexing the elbows until you feel a full biceps contraction; avoid swinging the torso or using shoulder elevation.
Pause 0–1 seconds at the top with tension on the biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until arms are nearly extended but elbows remain slightly bent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form and avoiding momentum.
exercises.description: Seated lever biceps curl performed on a machine that isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps; the user sits upright and pulls machine handles toward the shoulders by flexing the elbows. Controlled concentric and eccentric phases minimize momentum and emphasize the biceps contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
